📦 Olist E-Commerce Analytics

End-to-end SQL analytics + ML project on the real Brazilian marketplace dataset

1.55M rows 9 tables 55 SQL queries 3 ML models Streamlit + HTML dashboards

1 · 📊 Business Problem

Olist is Brazil's largest department-store marketplace, connecting thousands of small sellers to the country's biggest e-commerce platforms. Between Sep 2016 and Oct 2018 they processed ~100k orders from 96k unique customers, generating R$ 15.8M in GMV.

The four decisions leadership needs to make:
  1. Which customer segments to prioritise for retention?
  2. Which states / product categories to double-down on?
  3. Where are logistics bottlenecks silently destroying CSAT?
  4. Which customers will churn next — and which are worth saving?

Monthly GMV & order volume — steady scaling through 2017, plateau in 2018

Health-beauty, watches, sports lead category revenue

4 · 👥 Customer Segments (RFM in pure SQL)

6 named segments derived from R × F × M quintiles using SQL NTILE(5) window functions

5 · 🚚 Logistics & CX

Northern states lag SLA — freight ops target list
Late deliveries drop reviews from 4.30 → 2.85 (−1.45 ⭐)

6 · ♻️ Cohort Retention

Only ~3% of any cohort orders again in the same 12-month window — massive CLV opportunity

7 · 🤖 ML Model 1 — Churn Prediction (XGBoost)

Result: ROC-AUC 0.8945 · Accuracy 80.5% · Avg-Precision 0.9469  on 23,756 hold-out customers.
ROC curve
Precision-Recall curve
Confusion matrix (balanced via class weights)
Feature importance

Technical details

  • Target: churn_flag = 1 if no order in ≥ 180 days as of 2018-10-01
  • 9 features engineered in SQL (Q54): frequency, monetary, tenure, on-time rate, avg review, category diversity, etc.
  • XGBoost — 400 trees, depth 5, scale_pos_weight = neg/pos for imbalance
  • 75/25 stratified split, seeded random_state=42

8 · 🧩 ML Model 2 — Customer Segmentation (KMeans on RFM)

4 clusters visualised on log-Recency × log-Monetary
Segment sizes — High-Value is small but very valuable

9 · ⭐ ML Model 3 — Review-Score Regression

Predicted vs actual review score — hexbin density

Model card

  • Algorithm: Gradient Boosting Regressor (250 trees, depth 4)
  • MAE: 0.9048 stars (target 1-5)
  • R²: 0.2224
  • Business use: alert seller ops when predicted score < 3.5

11 · 🧠 Showcase SQL Queries

Q01. Headline KPIs

SELECT
    ROUND(SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value), 2)      AS gmv_brl,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id)                       AS orders,
    COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_unique_id)             AS unique_customers,
    ROUND(SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) / COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id), 2) AS aov_brl
FROM orders o
JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
JOIN customers  c   ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.order_status NOT IN ('canceled','unavailable');

Q14. RFM segments via NTILE

WITH agg AS (
  SELECT c.customer_unique_id,
         MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_order,
         COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id)      AS frequency,
         SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS monetary
  FROM orders o
  JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
  WHERE o.order_status NOT IN ('canceled','unavailable')
  GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id
), rfm AS (
  SELECT customer_unique_id,
         CAST(julianday('2018-10-01') - julianday(last_order) AS INT) AS recency_days,
         frequency, monetary,
         NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY julianday(last_order) DESC) AS R,
         NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY frequency DESC)             AS F,
         NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY monetary DESC)              AS M
  FROM agg
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN R<=2 AND F<=2 AND M<=2 THEN 'Champions'
    WHEN R<=2 AND F<=3           THEN 'Loyal'
    WHEN R<=2                    THEN 'Potential Loyalist'
    WHEN R=3                     THEN 'At Risk'
    WHEN R>=4 AND F<=2           THEN 'Hibernating'
    ELSE 'Lost'
  END AS segment,
  COUNT(*) AS customers,
  ROUND(AVG(monetary),2) AS avg_monetary,
  ROUND(AVG(recency_days),1) AS avg_recency
FROM rfm GROUP BY segment ORDER BY customers DESC;

Q18. Cohort retention

WITH firsts AS (
  SELECT c.customer_unique_id,
         strftime('%Y-%m', MIN(o.order_purchase_timestamp)) AS cohort
  FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id
),
activity AS (
  SELECT c.customer_unique_id,
         strftime('%Y-%m', o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS active_month
  FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id, active_month
)
SELECT f.cohort, a.active_month,
       COUNT(DISTINCT a.customer_unique_id) AS active_customers
FROM firsts f
JOIN activity a ON a.customer_unique_id = f.customer_unique_id
GROUP BY f.cohort, a.active_month
ORDER BY f.cohort, a.active_month;

Q52. Delivery lateness vs review

SELECT CAST(julianday(o.order_delivered_customer_date)
            - julianday(o.order_estimated_delivery_date) AS INT) AS lateness_days,
       ROUND(AVG(r.review_score),2) AS avg_score,
       COUNT(*) AS n
FROM orders o JOIN order_reviews r ON r.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY lateness_days HAVING n > 30
ORDER BY lateness_days;

Q54. ML training feature view

WITH base AS (
  SELECT c.customer_unique_id,
         COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS frequency,
         SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS monetary,
         AVG(oi.price) AS avg_item_price,
         AVG(oi.freight_value) AS avg_freight,
         AVG(r.review_score) AS avg_review,
         MAX(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_ts,
         MIN(o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS first_ts,
         AVG(CASE WHEN o.order_delivered_customer_date <= o.order_estimated_delivery_date
                  THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time_rate,
         COUNT(DISTINCT p.product_category_name) AS n_categories
  FROM orders o
  JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
  JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
  JOIN products p ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
  LEFT JOIN order_reviews r ON r.order_id = o.order_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_unique_id
)
SELECT customer_unique_id, frequency, ROUND(monetary,2) AS monetary,
       ROUND(avg_item_price,2) AS avg_item_price,
       ROUND(avg_freight,2) AS avg_freight,
       ROUND(avg_review,2)  AS avg_review,
       CAST(julianday('2018-10-01')-julianday(last_ts) AS INT) AS recency_days,
       CAST(julianday(last_ts)-julianday(first_ts) AS INT)     AS tenure_days,
       ROUND(on_time_rate,3) AS on_time_rate,
       n_categories,
       CASE WHEN julianday('2018-10-01')-julianday(last_ts) > 180 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS churn_flag
FROM base;
